title: Babel
content:
Oh, God did cunningly, there at Babel - 
    Not mere tongues dividing, but soul from soul,
    So that never again should men be able
    To fashion one infinite, towering whole.
meaning:
Lola Ridge's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Babel"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
